checks.a_analysis: I examined the entire output JSON against the source OCR provided. All headings, paragraphs, and list items in the output have a direct basis in the source document. The title and image alt text are expected AI-generated additions. There are no invented section headings (other than those derived from the document itself) or hallucinated body text.

The source document contains very limited contact information (e.g., author name). I verified the author name 'Derek Graham' and the report metadata. No phone numbers, email addresses, or URLs were present in either the source or the output. No fabrications were detected.

Comparing the source document pages to the output JSON sections revealed that a large block of content is missing. Specifically, pages 14 through 22 are absent. These pages contain major sections such as 'Case Study – Charlotte/Meck Bus Stop Consolidation', 'Bus Routes Before/After Stop Consolidation', 'Improvement from Stop Consolidation' (including its data table), 'School Boundary Planning', 'State Funding for School Transportation', 'Number of Buses Operated' (including the graph), 'Other applications', 'Data for Growth Projections', and 'School Assignment – balancing equity...'. While an omission block exists for pages 4-10, the gap from page 14 to 22 is not marked and represents a silent loss of data.
checks.b_evidence_source: Source pages 14 ('Case Study – Charlotte/Meck Bus Stop Consolidation') through 22 ('School Assignment – balancing equity based on median income').
checks.b_evidence_output: The output JSON skips from the content of page 13 (within section 'Bus Routing Information') directly to the section 'Future Growth Maps – projecting likely hotspots of student growth' (page 23).
checks.b_evidence_location: Between the section 'Bus Routing Information' and 'Future Growth Maps...', pages 14-22.

checks.a_analysis: The output contains two tables. The first table (page 11, 'Bus Route Stop Details') captures all the data rows from the source, although some rows trailing the 'Warning' marker are reformatted as paragraphs. The second table (page 16) is missing entirely because the entire page was dropped (recorded under C3). C4 applies to truncated tables; since the P11 table is complete in terms of information, this check passes.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The sections that are present in the output (Pages 1-3, 11-13, 23-24) generally contain all the text from the source. The omission of entire sections (Pages 14-22) is covered under C3. No partial omissions within the retained sections were found.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: I performed a textual comparison of the data in the tables and paragraphs. The data values (e.g., student counts, mileages, bus run numbers) match the source. The reformatting of table cells into paragraphs for certain route directions is considered acceptable. No changes in meaning or incorrect numbers were found.

checks.a_analysis: The cover page (Page 1) is repeated at the end of the document (Page 24) in the source PDF. The output JSON faithfully reproduces this repetition by including the same content in the first and last sections. This is an intentional repetition in the source and not a conversion defect.

The heading hierarchy and document segmentation are severely compromised. The output merges the content of eleven distinct pages (Pages 11 through 21) into a single section ('Bus Routing Information'). The primary titles for Pages 12 through 21 (e.g., 'NCDOT Railroad Crossing Report', 'Bus Run Optimization', 'School Boundary Planning', etc.) have been demoted to Level 2 headings and placed as subsections within the Page 11 container. In the source, each of these is a standalone page with a primary title, necessitating its own section at the root level of the hierarchy.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 12: NCDOT Railroad Crossing Report; Page 13: Bus Run Optimization; Page 17: School Boundary Planning
checks.b_evidence_output: The output contains Level 2 headings for these titles ('NCDOT Railroad Crossing Report', 'Bus Run Optimization', 'School Boundary Planning') all nested within the 'Bus Routing Information' section (reading_order: 11).

checks.a_analysis: The table on Page 11 ('Bus Route Stop Details') and Page 16 ('Comparison of Opening Day...') are captured with correct structural fidelity. The column and row alignments match the visual representation in the source PDF, including the use of spanning rows for table titles.

The logical flow of the document is broken because an entire page of content is missing from the output. The sections array follows a reading_order sequence of 1, 2, 3... 11... 13, 14. There is no section corresponding to logical block 12 (which should contain Page 22: 'School Assignment – balancing equity based on median income'). Additionally, the massive merger of Pages 11-21 into one section disrupts the intended organization of the document.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 22: School Assignment – balancing equity based on median income
checks.b_evidence_output: The sections array skips from reading_order 11 ('Bus Routing Information') to reading_order 13 ('Future Growth Maps'), entirely omitting the content for Page 22.

checks.a_analysis: The list on Page 3 ('A Bit of History') is correctly converted into a four-item unordered list. Other lists in the document (e.g., Pages 18 and 20) are also correctly captured within their (improperly nested) subsections.
The source document contains no URLs or interactive links. The output correctly reflects this by not injecting any fabricated links.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output faithfully reflects the visual organization of the source PDF by following the sequential order of the slides. The conversion into a single-column scrolling layout is an appropriate adaptation for web content and maintains the logical flow of the original presentation.

Tables present in the PDF, notably the comparison data on page 16, are rendered correctly in the HTML. The conversion has also successfully identified and formatted report text from page 11 into a readable table ('Bus Route Stop Details'). In both cases, the tables have clear row/column structures and are easy to read.

A significant number of meaningful images are missing from the HTML output. This includes almost all critical graphics such as maps (pages 12, 13, 14, 15, 17, 22, 23), charts (page 19), diagrams (page 8), and software screenshots (pages 7, 9, 10). Additionally, some images are rendered as solid green placeholder boxes (e.g., pages 6 and 11), indicating broken assets. Since this is a GIS-focused presentation, the loss of these visuals significantly impacts the document's utility.
checks.b_evidence_source: Meaningful visual content including the TIMS System diagram (page 8), NCDOT Railroad Crossing Report map (page 12), and Number of Buses Operated chart (page 19).
checks.b_evidence_output: The corresponding images are absent from their respective sections in the HTML. For example, the map on page 12 is missing entirely, and the photo on page 11 is replaced by a green box.

checks.a_analysis: No visual content duplication was observed beyond what is present in the original document (where the first slide is repeated at the end).
